description: Fix plugin development frictions discovered during the Discord plugin build: improve scaffold templates (package.json, isReady, prettier, README), and add iframe fallback to SDK getLocalStorage for apps that delete window.localStorage

userStories.title: Fix scaffold package.json to match established plugin conventions
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded package.json to match the established plugin conventions so that I don't have to manually adjust it after creation
acceptanceCriteria: Scaffolded package.json has a `check` script: `npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check`
Scaffolded `dev` script uses shell background (`&`) instead of `concurrently`: `tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput & opentabs-plugin build --watch`
Scaffolded package.json includes `publishConfig: { access: 'restricted' }` for scoped packages or omits it for unscoped
Scaffolded package.json includes a top-level `description` field matching the opentabs.description
The `//` comment field inside the opentabs object is removed
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts`, function `generatePackageJson` (lines 77-132). Changes needed:

1. Add `description: args.description` to the pkg object (currently only inside the opentabs field)
2. Add `check` script: `'npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check'`
3. Change `dev` script from `concurrently --names tsc,build --prefix-colors blue,green "tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput" "opentabs-plugin build --watch"` to `tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput & opentabs-plugin build --watch`
4. Remove `concurrently` from devDependencies (around line 120)
5. Add `publishConfig: { access: 'restricted' }` to the pkg object
6. Remove the `'//': 'Optional: place icon.svg ...'` comment from the opentabs field

Do NOT change the package name to include a scope — that's a user choice. The scaffold creates unscoped names by design.

Also check `platform/create-plugin/` which uses the same scaffold — it imports from `@opentabs-dev/cli/scaffold`.

userStories.title: Improve scaffold prettier config to match established plugin conventions
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded .prettierrc to match the established plugin conventions so that code I write matches the expected formatting
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts`, constant `PRETTIERRC_CONTENT` at lines 191-202. Add the two missing properties to match what the Slack plugin uses in `plugins/slack/.prettierrc`:
- `"bracketSameLine": true`
- `"htmlWhitespaceSensitivity": "strict"`

userStories.title: Improve scaffold isReady with auth extraction examples and polling pattern
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded isReady method to include a practical polling pattern example so that I know how to properly handle SPA hydration delay
acceptanceCriteria: Scaffolded isReady method body still returns false by default (safe to ship without changes)
Comments above isReady include an example of the polling/wait pattern used by production plugins
Comments mention the iframe localStorage fallback for apps that delete window.localStorage

userStories.title: Add auth extraction patterns section to scaffold README
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded README to include guidance on common auth extraction patterns so that I know how to discover and extract auth tokens from different web apps
acceptanceCriteria: Scaffolded README includes a section on authentication extraction
Section covers localStorage, cookies, page globals, and webpack module stores
Section mentions the iframe localStorage fallback for apps that delete window.localStorage
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts`, function `generateReadme` (lines 210-353). Add a new section after 'Adding Tools' and before or after 'Shared Schemas'. Title it '## Authentication' or '## Auth Extraction'. Content should cover:

1. **localStorage**: `localStorage.getItem('token')` — most common pattern
2. **Cookies**: `document.cookie` — check for session tokens
3. **Page globals**: `window.__APP_STATE__`, `window.boot_data`, etc.
4. **Iframe fallback**: Some apps (like Discord) delete `window.localStorage` — create a hidden same-origin iframe and use `iframe.contentWindow.localStorage`
5. **SPA hydration**: Auth may not be available immediately on page load — implement polling in `isReady()`

Keep it concise — 20-30 lines max. Reference the SDK utilities: `getLocalStorage`, `getPageGlobal`, `getCookie`.

userStories.title: Add iframe fallback to SDK getLocalStorage for apps that delete window.localStorage
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the SDK's getLocalStorage utility to automatically try an iframe fallback when window.localStorage is undefined so that plugins work on apps like Discord that delete the property
acceptanceCriteria: getLocalStorage returns the correct value even when window.localStorage is undefined
The iframe fallback creates a hidden same-origin iframe, reads localStorage, and removes the iframe
The fallback is only attempted when the direct access fails (not on every call)
The function still returns null on SecurityError (sandboxed iframes)
